display_name: BGP communities not propagating to a neighbor because send-community is not configured
context: Route-policy or route-map attaches communities to prefixes, but the receiving neighbor does not see them
observation: A prefix is advertised to a BGP neighbor with the correct route-policy applied, but 'show bgp' on the receiving side shows no communities attached to the prefix
naive_interpretation: The route-map or route-policy is not working — the community is not being set correctly, or there is a bug preventing community attachment
correct_interpretation: By default, IOS-XE, NX-OS, and IOS-XR strip all communities (standard, extended, and large) when advertising prefixes to eBGP and iBGP neighbors. The 'send-community' configuration must be explicitly enabled per neighbor or peer-group for the communities to be included in BGP UPDATE messages. The route-map IS setting the community on the local router — but the community is removed from the UPDATE before it is sent to the neighbor. This is not a bug; it is the default behavior per RFC 1997. On IOS-XE/NX-OS, 'send-community' covers standard communities only; 'send-community extended' and 'send-community both' are needed for extended communities (used by EVPN, MPLS VPN, etc.). On IOS-XR, 'send-community-ebgp' and 'send-extended-community-ebgp' are the equivalent knobs for eBGP peers.
why: RFC 1997 defines communities as an optional transitive attribute, but vendor implementations default to NOT sending them — the operator must opt in per neighbor
Extended communities (RT, SOO) used by EVPN and MPLS VPN require 'send-community extended' in addition to standard 'send-community' — configuring only one type leaves the other stripped
The stripping happens at the UPDATE generation stage, AFTER route-map processing — so 'show bgp' on the local router shows the community, but the neighbor never receives it
This default exists because communities can influence routing decisions on remote ASes — sending them indiscriminately could cause unintended routing behavior

confirmation_commands.command: show bgp ipv4 unicast neighbors <neighbor-ip> | include send-community
confirmation_commands.purpose: Check if send-community is enabled for this neighbor — if not shown, communities are being stripped
Verify the community IS set on the local RIB — if present locally but missing on the neighbor, the problem is send-community

confirmation_commands.command: show ip bgp neighbors <neighbor-ip> | include send-community
confirmation_commands.purpose: Check if send-community standard/extended/both is configured for this peer
Check for send-community-ebgp and send-extended-community-ebgp configuration

traps: Debugging the route-map or route-policy when it IS working correctly — the community is set locally but stripped before sending
Configuring 'send-community' but forgetting 'send-community extended' when EVPN or VPN route-targets are needed — standard and extended are separate knobs on IOS-XE/NX-OS
Checking the local BGP table and seeing the community, then assuming the neighbor must also see it — the UPDATE generation is where stripping occurs
Not realizing that 'send-community' must be configured per-neighbor or peer-group — there is no global knob to enable it for all peers
On IOS-XR, using 'send-community' (iBGP syntax) instead of 'send-community-ebgp' for eBGP peers
